steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Start from $49 = \frac{2K}{v^2}$. Multiply both sides by $v^2$ to clear the denominator: $49v^2 = 2K$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Divide both sides by 49 to isolate $v^2$: $v^2 = \frac{2K}{49}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Since $v$ is positive, take the positive square root of both sides: $v = \sqrt{\frac{2K}{49}}$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• The matching choice: $v=\sqrt{\frac{2K}{49}}$. Matches the derived expression exactly. A is correct.
  • $v=\sqrt{\frac{49}{2K}}$. This has the fraction inverted; it would follow from $v^2 = \frac{49}{2K}$, which is wrong. Eliminated.
  • $v=\frac{49^{x}}{2K}$. Introduces an exponent variable $x$ that does not appear in the problem and does not follow from the algebra. Eliminated.
  • $v=\frac{2K}{49^{x}}$. Also introduces $x$ and is not derived from the equation; there is no step in the solution that produces a quotient of this form. Eliminated.
